key_insight: MR-GVNO learns Mindlin-Reissner plate responses on irregular geometries directly from boundary point clouds and physics-based energy loss, eliminating the need for labeled FEM solutions.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate prediction of plate deflection and rotation across varying shapes, materials, and loads for design and optimization workflows.
method: Geometry-aware neural operator with boundary point clouds for irregular domains
Separate encoders for material fields, pressure loads, and scalar parameters
Cross-attention with query points plus variational physics-informed training from discretized total potential energy
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tests on single-hole, double-hole, and L-shaped plates with homogeneous/heterogeneous materials and uniform/random loads, achieving millisecond-level full-field inference and good cross-geometry generalization.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate point-cloud geometry and physics discretization, and generalization may weaken for geometries or loading regimes outside the tested set.
paper_title: MR-GVNO: A Geometry-Aware Variational Physics-Informed Neural Operator for Mindlin-Reissner Plates on Irregular Domains
